claim_boundary: Ticket 91 proves one exact coordinate theorem and one proof-route no-go but solves none of the four open problems.

attempts.attempt: Replace a single limiting ghost by the full target-avoiding invariant set and require escape from that set.

bounded_result.candidate_theorem: Characterize and escape the full sign-preserving invariant cone, not one limiting kernel.

attempts.obstruction: No target-specific invariant-set conjugacy or escape theorem was proved.

attempts.claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.

attempts.proof_or_counterexample_mode: exact growing-precision tail conjugacy plus invariant-set route correction
attempts.attempt: Determine what the beta ghost represents and whether separation from it can force the missing 00 recurrence.

exact_tail_identity.fixed_exponent: Let R in Z_2 be the odd solution of 3^(R+1)=-7 and write R=r_H+2^(H+1)t_H.
exact_tail_identity.limiting_congruence: With alpha=lim A_H and gamma=7alpha, e_H=gamma t_H (mod 2^(H+2)).

limiting_conjugacy.map: F(e)=e/2 for even e and F(e)=(e+beta)/2 for odd e, where beta=-gamma.
limiting_conjugacy.conjugacy: Multiplication by gamma conjugates the one-sided binary tail shift to F, losing one output bit per step.
limiting_conjugacy.ghost_identification: The fixed point e=beta is exactly t=-1, the all-one future tail.
limiting_conjugacy.shadow_identity: min(v2(e_H-beta),H+2)=min(v2(t_H+1),H+2); this is only the initial run length of future one bits.

golden_mean_obstruction.tail_set: G={one-sided binary tails with no occurrence of 00}.
golden_mean_obstruction.error_set: K=gamma*G is invariant under the limiting normalized-error map.
golden_mean_obstruction.size: At depth n, G has F_(n+2) words; in the limit it is uncountable and has positive entropy.
golden_mean_obstruction.single_ghost_no_go: Separating the actual orbit from beta only proves that a future zero occurs. TICKET87 already proves infinitely many zero bits, but this does not force adjacent zeros.
golden_mean_obstruction.required_escape: The additive-two target requires the actual tail orbit to leave G, equivalently the error orbit to leave K, infinitely often.

proof_chain: Write the fixed 2-adic exponent as its certified prefix plus a 2-adic future tail.
Substitute this decomposition into 3^(R+1)=-7 and solve exactly for the normalized error.
Expand one 2-adic binomial order and use A_H=alpha mod 2^(H+2) to obtain growing-precision conjugacy.
Identify beta=-gamma with the all-one tail and identify beta-shadow depth with the next zero-bit waiting time.
Conjugate the full binary shift, not only one fixed point, to the limiting error map.
Map every no-00 tail into the invariant set K=gamma*G and retain its Fibonacci growth and positive entropy.
Reject single-ghost separation as a route to 00 recurrence and replace it with target-specific escape from K.

discarded_routes.counteredge: beta is only the all-one point inside the much larger invariant set gamma*G.
The alternating tail 1010... has infinitely many zeros and no 00.
Every finite legal cylinder extends to an infinite no-00 tail.

error_tail_invariant_set_audit.retained_route: Exploit the specific arithmetic relation R=log_2adic(-7)/log_2adic(3)-1 to prove its shift orbit exits the no-00 golden-mean set infinitely often.
error_tail_invariant_set_audit.candidate_theorem: GoldenMeanInvariantSetEscape: the tail shift orbit of the fixed exponent R leaves G infinitely often, equivalently R contains 00 infinitely often.
error_tail_invariant_set_audit.proof_boundary: TICKET91 proves an exact growing-precision coordinate theorem and corrects TICKET90's next route. It does not prove 00 recurrence, an additive-two infinite delay theorem, or Collatz.
error_tail_invariant_set_audit.novelty_boundary: The tail-error conjugacy packaging is a candidate PrimeProject contribution pending independent review; the golden-mean shift itself is standard symbolic dynamics.

attempts.obstruction: Separating the actual orbit from beta only proves that a future zero occurs. TICKET87 already proves infinitely many zero bits, but this does not force adjacent zeros.
attempts.candidate_theorem: GoldenMeanInvariantSetEscape: the tail shift orbit of the fixed exponent R leaves G infinitely often, equivalently R contains 00 infinitely often.
attempts.next_experiment: Build TICKET92 around arithmetic escape from the full golden-mean invariant set.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Collatz proof and no certified divergent Collatz counterexample.
